title: FIX-467: Bug Creating First Library Ticket — Root Cause & Fix Walkthrough
description: This tour walks through the complete lifecycle of FIX-467, a bug where creating the first library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts after several hours of waiting. The root cause was that the system was incorrectly using the user's authentication token instead of the built-in Helix system token when creating repositories — specifically for NetSuite file cabinet repos. Multiple agent runs were required to surface and fully resolve this issue. The tour follows investigation in data-flow order: from initial user report, through agent triage runs, to the final corrected token-selection logic and verification artifacts.

steps.description: This is the entry point: the original ticket FIX-467 filed by the user. The user reported that creating a new library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts after several hours of waiting. Screenshots were attached as evidence. A critical constraint is noted here — the agent's available database is not an exact copy of production, but the fix must ensure the new library is created regardless. This ticket context file seeds the entire investigation and sets the scope: the problem is environment-specific (staging) and involves the artifact creation pipeline.
steps.title: Step 1: Original Ticket Report (FIX-467)

steps.description: In this second run, the Helix agent acknowledged the ticket and began triage. The discussion section records the first agent response at 2026-05-15T00:13:39Z. No code changes were landed yet — this run focused on understanding the failure mode from attached screenshots and available database state. Data flow at this stage: user report → agent acknowledgment → environment inspection. The agent is scoping which subsystem is responsible for artifact creation.
steps.title: Step 2: Agent Acknowledges and Begins Triage

steps.description: This run produced the first attempted fix — the agent believed it had resolved the issue and posted verification screenshots. However, the fix only addressed a surface symptom. The user followed up at 2026-05-15T03:30:52Z clarifying that the real problem is token selection: for NetSuite file cabinet repos, the system was using the user's own auth token instead of the built-in Helix system token that should be used for repository creation. This is the pivotal moment where the actual root cause is identified.
steps.title: Step 3: First Fix Attempt — Surface Symptom Only

steps.description: This run captures the agent's detailed acknowledgment of the true root cause: the token-selection logic was incorrectly forwarding the requesting user's credential to the repository creation call, rather than switching to the Helix system token. The agent explains the precise failure path and outlines the corrective approach — the system must detect when the repo type is a NetSuite file cabinet repo and override token selection to use the Helix built-in token regardless of the initiating user context.
steps.title: Step 4: Root Cause Confirmed — Wrong Token for NetSuite Repos

steps.description: This CodeTour artifact was generated during the run that first fully understood the root cause. It documents the investigation trail and begins mapping the token-selection fix to specific code locations. Reviewing this intermediate walkthrough shows how the agent's understanding evolved: earlier steps focus on symptom observation, while later steps target the token-override logic that must be applied at repository creation time for NetSuite file cabinet repos.
steps.title: Step 5: Intermediate CodeTour — Investigation Artifact

steps.description: A refined CodeTour generated in the next run, incorporating the corrected understanding of the token-selection bug. This artifact reflects a cleaner narrative of the data flow: user initiates library creation → system determines repo type → if NetSuite file cabinet, token must be switched to Helix system token before the repository creation API call is made → artifact is produced. This walkthrough also begins to identify the specific files and functions in helix-cli responsible for token selection.
steps.title: Step 6: Refined CodeTour — Corrected Token-Selection Data Flow

steps.description: The final run's ticket file contains the most complete discussion history, including the agent's full explanation of why the previous fix was insufficient and what the correct fix entails. The agent confirms the fix was applied: the token-selection logic now correctly routes NetSuite file cabinet repo creation through the Helix system token, bypassing the user's credential entirely. This ensures artifact creation succeeds regardless of the initiating user's permissions or token state.
steps.title: Step 7: Final Run — Fix Applied and Verified

steps.description: This is the final CodeTour produced after the complete fix was applied. It serves as the canonical walkthrough for FIX-467, documenting the full investigation lifecycle, the root cause (wrong token used for NetSuite file cabinet repo creation), and the corrected code path. The major code paths for library ticket creation are confirmed clean: the system now properly differentiates between user-token contexts and Helix system-token contexts at the repository creation boundary, and all verification screenshots confirm artifacts are produced successfully in the staging environment.
steps.title: Step 8: Final CodeTour — Summary and Code Paths Confirmed Clean
